name: Totoya Kaimuki
tagline: Sixteen-seat kaisendon counter where the sashimi is piled so thick you can barely see the rice — get there early or join the wait.
mustOrder: kaisen don — the signature sashimi rice bowl with daily fish selection
negitori don — torched salmon belly over rice
miso soup — yuzu or ginger-infused, not an afterthought
parking: Street parking on 12th Ave and side streets; competitive during lunch rush
talkStory: Opened in late 2024 on 12th Ave in Kaimuki by a Japanese chef and instantly became one of the hardest lunch reservations on Oahu. Only 16 counter seats with an open kitchen where you watch your kaisen don being built with exceptional daily-sourced fish. Lines form before opening. The move is weekday 1:30pm or late dinner for shorter waits. Already at 4.8 Google stars with 200+ reviews — one of the highest-rated new restaurants on the island.
